item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ll admit I initially downvoted the skip, as there’s very little content here, besides description of a simple object. But the fridge logic hit me today, the Foundation has a lot of these things, but is only ever gonna save one person. Meaning that 1) no one is using their influence in the Foundation to save themselves, not even members of O5 and 2) there’s something about that one person to make them more important than anyone else in the world and the skip doesn’t even hint at why. And that I find clever and intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm amazed at the creativity here. For some reason, the combination of an object that does a decent job in an over-the-top way, paired with the foundation proving its intellect by using a strange object to the best of it's ability, puts me in awe. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hooked on these puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is simultaneously incredibly stupid and incredibly useful. you get an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I get it. Thank you for answering my questions, I guess I were overthinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 14-degree thing is just jargon that I made up. It doesn't actually mean anything.

The Least Differences Principle similarly lacks an explicit definition, but the point I meant to convey with it was "things won't be different from the baseline unless that's necessary for the effect to work".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In most cases, the difference between the origin universe and the destination universe is minimal (<14°)

I may sound stupid, but, what does it mean? The temperature change in the destination universe is of less than 14º? The hooks are rotated 14º to the left or to the right? Or is it a weird thing about open and closed universes, and how the sum of the three angles of a triangle is of less tan 180º? Maybe that degrees are a scientific term for some facts about the multiverse structure only known by the Foundation? I'm confused, I don't know if I'm missing something.

Also, what is the Least Differences Principle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In this setting, cross-universe communication is possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think that it's because enough researchers have come from other universes that they've pieced it together through interviews. I also like the idea that there are now multiple versions of some test subject at the same facility as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's meant to press on the strings to adjust the tuning without messing with the knobs. It's mainly a quick way to step up your guitar's base notes so that you can switch musical keys quickly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious as to how the Foundation knows so much about the alternate universes this device takes you to. Is this information acquired solely from interviewing people who have come here from those universes, or have researchers actually managed to return to their native universe by cycling through enough bathrooms?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thaumiel that isn't Big, Scary, Omnipotent Being From Outer Space. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah. I didn't expect as many people to recognize it as, apparently, have. Probably because the actual functionality of the thing is somewhat obtuse to me, as a non-guitar player.

I've debated about having the article specifically call it a capo, but I do enjoy the headcanon that, in the universe where this article was written, capos don't exist. Plus the rating seems to be doing fine regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are all hooking my patience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is…is the picture a capo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I accept this headcannon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Headcannon: it's clearly the head researcher/archivist/containment specialist/administrator/etc. responsible for K-class planning: the only knowledge worth bringing out of a failing universe is what caused it to fail, and this is the person who can best help prevent it from repeating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's so, so incredibly dumb as a concept, but somehow it manages to work as a perfectly plausible and useful item. A dumb little guitar tool used as interuniversal travel? I can't say I haven't see things similar, but I certainly can't say that I've seen something the same. Well done, well made.

item.title: oh neat it's +100
item.content:encoded: look

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that is entirely the point of codenames and need-to-know access controls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sums up my rationale better than I could have.

In-universe, it can be assumed that anyone who has access to this document either A) is cleared to know who the VIP is, and would thus have access to the files that say who the VIP is, and thus it requires no elaboration here, or B) is not cleared to know who the VIP is, and thus no elaboration is needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm happy we finally have a small-scale Thaumiel. I mean, it has to be something impressive, or else the Foundation wouldn't use it. But I think you found the most specific, smallest li'l item the Foundation would conceivably decide to use. And it's why I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually think the mystery does help.

He's presenting the idea of a very easy to use and reliable dimension hoping device, but it can only be used by one person at a time.

Then he presents the idea that in the entirety of the Foundation, there is only really one person relevant enough to use it in a K-class event.

By not specifying who this is or what their role is it invites the reader to ask, who the hell could that possibly be. It gets you thinking. Lets you come up with your own ideas of who isn't necessarily the important person in the Foundation but the most useful in a end of the world situation.

If he were to answer the question of who the VIP is, no matter what he comes up with, I'm pretty sure it wouldn't satisfy most people. This seems to me like the kind of thing best kept a mystery so that readers can fill with their head cannons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These puns are so bad that you deserve a left hook to the face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok that somehow makes it even better.

I bet this was made by the same guy who made the Cold Fusion Paper Towel Dispenser.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it looks like an oversight, and the mystery doesn't really help the article any.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…So the Foundation doesn't know what a capo is?

I don't expect my readers to know what a capo is. Before it showed up on google image search, I'd never seen such a device in my entire life. What am I supposed to do, get a picture of a manmade object that DOESN'T exist?

EDIT: You know what? I'm actually going to go with that. The Foundation in this universe has never heard of a capo, or if it has, it doesn't look anything like this. Multi-U stuff makes this plausible!

And am I wrong, or is "VIP-576" never defined?

It's not defined, and it never will be. Why kill the mystery?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…So the Foundation doesn't know what a capo is? Is this document supposed to originate from a universe in which frets must always be depressed manually? I couldn't believe this at all.

And am I wrong, or is "VIP-576" never defined?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have any hang ups about good hook puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A capo is the closest I could get to "weird-looking thing that has no obvious purpose". You're the first person to actually identify it, I think.

I also imagine the better Foundation sites having quadruply redundant power supplies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. The extrauniversal travel is neat, the idea is silly but well-explained, and I'm just damn fascinated by the Monomyth Protocol. Wondering who our VIP in question could be.

Although given the image of a capo, I was definitely expecting VIP-576 to be a bunch of bathroom-gathering universe-hopping folk musicians before I got further into it.

(Also fun imagining all the maintenance people having to pick up those hooks off the bathroom floor every time there's a power issue.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both of you should be ashamed of those puns. But I'll let you off the hook this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I need this in my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a pretty neat and novel Thaumiel, I like it a lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: kinchtheknifeblade

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm imagining a series of communications between and among the O5 council and the Ethics Committee on the best practices approach to this.

Who gets to be the VIP, though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THERE IS A BREACH! ALL VIPS GET TO THE TOILET

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems this SCP allows people to play hooky with their own universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't even…
This made me what the fuck and fuck the what hard. Take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has a good hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…I don't…

What the actual fuck. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quirkiest Thaumiel ever…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…. this is something made to make sure you always have a coathanger for when you need to use the restroom? And it does it by transporting you to an alternate universe.

Yep. It was inspired by my perpetual frustration at public bathrooms that don't have hangers. Why the fuck wouldn't you have a hanger? It's two bucks worth of metal!

Does it send you back to your universe after you've done your business?

Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is terrific. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…. this is something made to make sure you always have a coathanger for when you need to use the restroom? And it does it by transporting you to an alternate universe.

Does it send you back to your universe after you've done your business?

item.title: Hey losers in Recent Forum Posts: It's a bathroom-centric Thaumiel. Check it out.
item.content:encoded:
This is simultaneously the dumbest and sexiest idea I've had in a while.
